name: AI meeting summarisation and action tracking
description: Automatically summarises meetings, extracts action items, and assigns owners. No system integration required — works on recordings or transcripts.
value_statement: Saves 20–40 minutes per person per meeting day. High adoption because it makes meetings less painful.
prerequisite: Meeting recordings or live transcription. No data infrastructure required.
implementation_complexity: Low — no integration; standalone tool deployment
dq_notes: Best 'Not Ready' quick win. Demonstrates AI value without requiring any data or systems work. Use as confidence builder before bigger initiatives.

name: AI document classification and extraction
description: Classifies and extracts structured data from high-volume documents — invoices, purchase orders, contracts, compliance forms. Works on existing PDF/scanned document volumes.
value_statement: 60–80% reduction in manual document processing time. High ROI for any organisation processing 50+ documents per day.
prerequisite: Digital or scanned document volume. No system integration required at entry level.
implementation_complexity: Low-Medium — document pipeline setup; can run standalone or integrate with existing systems later
dq_notes: Viable even at Not Ready because it does not require integration or historical data. Often the right pilot for organisations with siloed systems — delivers value while foundations are being built.

name: AI internal knowledge base and Q&A assistant
description: Allows employees to ask natural language questions over internal documents, policies, procedures, and manuals. Reduces time spent searching and escalating basic queries.
value_statement: 30–50% reduction in time-to-answer for internal queries. Particularly valuable in regulated sectors with large compliance document libraries.
prerequisite: Digital document library (policies, manuals, SOPs). No integration required.
implementation_complexity: Low — RAG implementation over existing documents
dq_notes: Good first AI deployment for government and regulated sectors. Low risk, high adoption, visible value.

name: AI customer support first-response assistant
description: Handles first-response customer queries using historical support tickets and FAQ content. Escalates complex queries to human agents with context summary.
value_statement: 30–50% reduction in first-contact resolution time. Handles 40–60% of routine queries without human intervention.
prerequisite: Historical support ticket data (6+ months). CRM or helpdesk system with queryable history.
implementation_complexity: Medium — requires integration with helpdesk system
dq_notes: Very common first AI deployment. Viability depends entirely on ticket history quality — probe whether tickets are categorised and resolved or just closed without resolution notes.

name: AI demand forecasting
description: Predicts product demand by SKU, region, and time period using historical sales, seasonality, and external signals. Informs production planning and inventory positioning.
value_statement: 15–25% reduction in inventory holding costs. 10–20% reduction in stockouts and overproduction.
prerequisite: 12+ months of historical sales and production data, accessible in a queryable form. Integration between sales and inventory systems preferred.
implementation_complexity: Medium — data pipeline + model training + dashboard integration
dq_notes: Most requested manufacturing AI use case. Fails if historical data is in spreadsheets or if demand patterns have been disrupted by COVID/supply chain events — probe data continuity.

name: Predictive maintenance alerts
description: Predicts equipment failures before they occur using sensor data, maintenance logs, and operational parameters. Triggers maintenance work orders at optimal intervals.
value_statement: 20–35% reduction in unplanned downtime. 10–15% reduction in maintenance spend.
prerequisite: Equipment sensor data (IoT) or structured maintenance log history (18+ months). Accessible programmatically.
implementation_complexity: Medium-High — IoT data pipeline or log extraction + model training
cost_band_indicative: QAR 100K–300K implementation depending on equipment complexity
dq_notes: High-value but often overestimated feasibility. Many manufacturers have sensors but logs are on paper or in CMMS systems that can't export cleanly. Probe data accessibility specifically.

name: AI visual quality inspection
description: Computer vision system that inspects products on the production line for defects, inconsistencies, and non-conformance. Replaces or augments manual visual inspection.
value_statement: 40–60% improvement in defect detection rate. Near-zero false-negative rate at scale.
prerequisite: Camera infrastructure on production line. Labelled image dataset of defect types (minimum 1,000–2,000 labelled images per defect class). Integration with production control system.
dq_notes: Requires physical infrastructure investment alongside AI. Often quoted without the camera/hardware cost. Ensure total cost estimate includes infrastructure.

name: AI route optimisation
description: Dynamically optimises delivery routes based on real-time traffic, delivery windows, vehicle capacity, and driver availability.
value_statement: 8–15% reduction in fuel and distance. 15–25% improvement in on-time delivery.
prerequisite: GPS/telematics data. Order and delivery history (6+ months). Vehicle fleet data.
dq_notes: Often already partially solved by incumbent logistics software. Probe whether this is a genuine gap or a vendor feature they haven't enabled.

name: AI product recommendation engine
description: Personalised product recommendations based on customer purchase history, browsing behaviour, and segment profiles. Delivered via website, app, or email.
value_statement: 10–25% increase in average basket size. 15–30% improvement in campaign click-through rate.
prerequisite: Transactional purchase history (12+ months). Customer identity resolution. Digital channel integration (website or app).
implementation_complexity: High — customer data platform integration + real-time serving layer
dq_notes: Common e-commerce AI use case. Viability depends entirely on customer identity data quality — if most purchases are anonymous, recommendation accuracy drops significantly.

name: AI dynamic pricing
description: Real-time price optimisation based on demand signals, competitor pricing, inventory levels, and customer segment. Maximises margin without sacrificing volume.
value_statement: 3–8% gross margin improvement. Significant in high-volume, competitive markets.
prerequisite: Real-time inventory data. Competitor price feed. Customer segment data. Strong integration between pricing engine and channel systems.
implementation_complexity: Very High — real-time integration across multiple systems
dq_notes: Reserve for organisations with strong data foundations. Attempting this before Scale Ready typically produces a batch pricing tool, not a dynamic pricing engine.

name: AI credit risk scoring
description: ML-based credit scoring that augments or replaces rule-based credit decisions. Uses transaction history, bureau data, and behavioural signals.
value_statement: 10–20% improvement in default prediction accuracy. 15–30% reduction in manual underwriting time.
prerequisite: 2+ years of loan origination and performance data with outcome labels (default/no default). Access to bureau data. Regulatory approval pathway identified.
implementation_complexity: High — model + regulatory compliance + core banking integration
dq_notes: High-value but heavily regulated. Must check Central Bank AI/ML model governance requirements for the jurisdiction before committing to this use case.

name: AI fraud detection
description: Real-time transaction scoring to identify potentially fraudulent activity. Replaces or augments rules-based fraud monitoring systems.
value_statement: 30–50% improvement in fraud detection rate. 20–40% reduction in false positives (which reduces friction for legitimate customers).
prerequisite: Real-time transaction data feed. Historical labelled fraud cases (class-imbalanced — probe whether there are enough labelled fraud examples). Integration with core banking/payment system.
dq_notes: Class imbalance is the technical failure mode — most fraud datasets have <1% positive examples. Probe specifically whether there are enough labelled fraud cases to train on.

name: AI citizen query and service assistant
description: Handles citizen queries about government services, eligibility, application status, and procedures. Arabic and English. Integrated with service portal.
value_statement: 40–60% reduction in call centre volume for routine queries. Improved citizen satisfaction on after-hours access.
prerequisite: Digital service catalogue. Historical query/call data. Integration with citizen portal or service management system.
implementation_complexity: Medium — service integration + Arabic NLP tuning
dq_notes: High priority for Gulf government clients. Arabic language quality is the differentiator — probe whether existing NLP vendors have been tested on Gulf Arabic dialect.

name: AI document processing for government applications
description: Extracts and validates structured data from submitted government application documents (permits, licenses, grants). Flags missing information and routes to appropriate reviewer.
value_statement: 50–70% reduction in manual data entry. 30–50% reduction in application processing time.
prerequisite: Digital document submission. No system integration required at entry level.
dq_notes: Often the right first AI use case for government clients — high volume, visible value, low technical risk. Good proof point for broader AI adoption.

name: AI property valuation assistance
description: Automated valuation model that provides indicative property values based on comparable transactions, location features, and market trends. Supports human valuers rather than replacing them.
value_statement: 60–70% reduction in time-to-indicative-value. Enables higher valuation throughput without headcount growth.
prerequisite: Historical transaction data with property attributes (12+ months, sufficient volume). Market data feed.
implementation_complexity: Medium — data pipeline + model + workflow integration
dq_notes: Very relevant for QDB context. Key risk: data quality of historical transactions — probe whether transactions are recorded with full attribute data or just price and date.

name: AI revenue management and demand forecasting
description: Forecasts occupancy and optimal room rate by date, room type, and booking channel. Replaces static seasonal pricing with dynamic revenue management.
value_statement: 5–12% RevPAR improvement. Better channel mix optimisation.
prerequisite: Historical booking and revenue data (24+ months). Channel integration (PMS/OTA).
implementation_complexity: Medium — PMS integration + rate management system connection
dq_notes: Post-COVID demand patterns disrupted many hospitality datasets. Probe whether pre-2020 data is usable or whether the model needs to be trained on post-recovery data only.

name: AI energy consumption optimisation
description: Predicts and optimises energy consumption across facilities or distribution networks. Identifies inefficiency patterns and recommends or automates load balancing.
value_statement: 8–15% reduction in energy costs. Supports sustainability reporting.
prerequisite: Smart meter or sensor data at facility or grid level. 12+ months of consumption history.
implementation_complexity: Medium — IoT data pipeline or meter integration
dq_notes: High relevance in Qatar given energy cost context and sustainability mandates. Probe whether smart metering infrastructure is in place.

name: AI CV screening and candidate matching
description: Screens and ranks CVs against role requirements. Identifies top candidates and flags mismatches. Reduces recruiter time-to-shortlist.
value_statement: 60–70% reduction in CV screening time. More consistent application of role criteria.
prerequisite: Digital CV/application data. Role description library. ATS system integration.
implementation_complexity: Low-Medium — ATS integration + bias testing required
dq_notes: Bias risk is the governance issue here. Any deployment must include bias testing across gender, nationality, and age groups before going live. Check local labour law implications.
